steps.title: Identify the decisive signal
steps.content: The phrase "guilds were much more numerous relative to the population in some cities than in others" requires an example that contrasts two cities with different guild-to-population ratios.

steps.title: Match the data to the claim
steps.content:
The correct response directly compares Rouen (1 guild per 661 inhabitants) to Paris (1 guild per 4,511 inhabitants), showing a roughly 7-fold difference in how densely guilds were distributed—exactly the type of variation the sentence claims to illustrate.

The other choices fail to support the claim:
  • A falsely states that Bordeaux and Lyon had nearly equal numbers of inhabitants, which the table does not show.
  • B incorrectly claims Lyon had more guilds than Paris.
  • D provides only Paris's figures without contrasting them with another city.
That confirms C is correct.
